label: Recurrent neural networks

node.label: Recurrent neural networks
node.definition: A recurrent neural network updates a hidden state for each sequence element, reusing parameters through time to represent order-dependent computation.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Recurrent neural networks is to connect Recurrence & feedback state, Autoregressive / next-step prediction to downstream capabilities including LSTMs & GRUs.
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Recurrence & feedback state, Autoregressive / next-step prediction.

dependencies.rationale: Recurrent neural networks is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Recurrence & feedback state.
Recurrent neural networks is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Autoregressive / next-step prediction.
Recurrent neural networks follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.

sentence_units.text: A recurrent neural network updates a hidden state for each sequence element, reusing parameters through time to represent order-dependent computation.
The role of Recurrent neural networks is to connect Recurrence & feedback state, Autoregressive / next-step prediction to downstream capabilities including LSTMs & GRUs.

Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Recurrence & feedback state, Autoregressive / next-step prediction.
